0.q: cDNAs of two members of the CLC family chloride channels, SaCLCa2 and SaCLCc2, from euhalophyte S. altissima, have been cloned in addition to SaCLCa1, SaCLCc1, SaCLCd, SaCLCf, and SaCLCg

1.q: cDNAs of two members of the CLC family chloride channels, SaCLCa2 and SaCLCc2... cloned in addition to SaCLCa1, SaCLCc1, SaCLCd, SaCLCf, and SaCLCg

2.q: SaCLCa2 expression in roots and leaves was considerably lower in plants grown under nitrate deficit than at sufficient supply of nitrate; addition of KNO3 resulted in considerable expression induction of SaCLCa2, while KCl did not.

3.q: The complementation of the Δgef1 mutant phenotype with the SaClCc1 gene indicates the involvement of the SaClCc1 protein in the transport of Cl– ions.
